_comment: Pterodactyl Egg for Auralis — https://github.com/ybenyedder/auralis. Author: Youssef Ben yedder <volt@webtvmedia.net>
name: Auralis
author: volt@webtvmedia.net
description: Self-hosted, local-first music platform (Next.js). Scans a music folder, streams it, fetches lyrics and serves a web + native client UI. Builds from source on install and runs with `next start`.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
apt-get update
apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git ca-certificates python3 make g++
mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server
# The install container runs as root but the files belong to the server user, so
# git would refuse with 'dubious ownership' — trust the build dir explicitly.
git config --global --add safe.directory '*'
if [ -d .git ]; then
  echo 'Updating existing checkout...'
  git fetch --all
  git reset --hard origin/${GIT_BRANCH:-main}
  git clean -fd -e music -e data
else
  echo 'Cloning Auralis...'
  git clone ${GIT_ADDRESS:-https://github.com/ybenyedder/auralis.git} .
  git checkout ${GIT_BRANCH:-main}
fi
echo 'Installing dependencies...'
# The desktop/mobile build tooling (Electron binary ~250MB, Puppeteer) is not
# needed to run the web server — skip those heavy downloads so the install fits.
export ELECTRON_SKIP_BINARY_DOWNLOAD=1
export PUPPETEER_SKIP_DOWNLOAD=1
export NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
npm ci --no-audit --no-fund
echo 'Building...'
npm run build
echo 'Trimming dev dependencies to shrink the runtime footprint...'
npm prune --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund || true
mkdir -p music data
echo 'Auralis install complete. Upload your audio into the music/ folder.'

variables.name: Git Repository
variables.description: Source repository to clone and build.

variables.name: Music directory
variables.description: Folder scanned and streamed. Upload your audio here.

variables.name: Data directory
variables.description: Writable folder for the SQLite database, cover-art cache and session secret.

variables.name: Admin password
variables.description: Initial admin password. Leave blank to get a random one printed once to the console.
